cross_asset_summaries.credit: 初級市場保持暢通且高收益債指標走強，顯示風險偏好足以消化龐大供給。
cross_asset_summaries.dollar_commodities: 原油走低與美元轉弱，緩解通膨及融資壓力，而黃金仍然堅挺。
cross_asset_summaries.equities_volatility: 科技股領漲及波動率下降，將這波行情界定為供給壓力緩解，而非經濟衰退。

header.stance: 油價壓力緩解壓低中期實質殖利率，但長天期阻力仍在

zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 固定收益開場論點

6月15日收盤重新啟動了前一週末未能延續的油價壓力緩解行情，但漲勢僅呈現在對政策最敏感的區段。隨著美國與伊朗的臨時協議壓低原油價格，並降低市場認為聯準會近期須作出反應的必要性，美債價格一路走高至殖利率曲線中段。長債並未跟漲，盤中遠大於收盤的漲幅也在收市前消退。結果是溫和牛市趨陡，而非明確脫離高實質利率環境。

這項結果改變了 6月12日的解讀，卻未將其完全推翻。週五收盤時，即使油價下跌，政策溢價仍再度建立，原因是風險偏好具韌性，且對滯後通膨的疑慮超過地緣政治壓力緩解的影響。週一則加入了更具實質性的協議、能源價格再度下降及美國生產數據轉弱。交易員將 Fed 在 12月前升息25個基點的機率，從前一週已完全定價下調至約 74%。即便如此，殘存的機率及美債尾盤漲幅回吐，顯示市場仍將現貨油價轉便宜視為急性衝擊的緩解，而非累積價格壓力已消失的證據。

3條傳導管道界定了收盤格局。協議降低能源尾端風險並壓低實質殖利率，5 年期區段尤其明顯。美國製造業指標隨後進一步支持耐心觀望的理由，然而調查中的價格指標偏高、產能利用率穩定，加上 10 年期通膨補償小幅上升，使數據無法被解讀為鴿派。最後，股市強勁，加上 Nvidia 超大型債券發行獲得大量承接，顯示這波漲勢並非由成長恐慌或信用創造受損所驅動。即使 AI 相關資本需求持續構成對儲蓄資金的競爭，風險偏好仍足以為其提供融資。

反向力量來自短期政策窗口以外的存續期間。歐洲官員持續強調能源的第2輪效應，台灣資金緊張正在推升當地出清殖利率，而最長天期美債則收平。因此，本次收盤恢復了 6月11日局勢降溫邏輯的1部分，同時保留 6月12日的核心警告：石油協議可以為央行爭取時間，但固定收益市場要獲得持久緩解，仍取決於供給實際正常化、更廣泛的通膨降溫，以及公私部門發債能在不推升期限溢價的情況下完成出清的證據。

## 美債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構

美債漲勢集中於前端及殖利率曲線中段。2 年期殖利率下降 2 個基點至 4.07%，5 年期下降 3 個基點至 4.18%，10 年期下滑 1 個基點至 4.47%。30 年期殖利率持平於 4.97%。這種天期表現顯示，調整集中在預期政策約束及中期實質利率。最長存續期間所要求的補償並未出現相應下降，因為財政供給、全球替代選項及持續的通膨不確定性，對該區段的影響仍然更大。

殖利率曲線溫和牛市趨陡。2s10s 斜率擴大 1 個基點至 40，2s30s 擴大 2 個基點至 90。隨著 5 年期區段表現優於兩翼，2s5s10s 蝶式利差下降 3 個基點至-18，而 2s10s30s 蝶式利差維持-10。這樣的組合符合中期政策路徑重新定價，而非長債獨立上漲。儘管有地緣政治催化因素，殖利率曲線的整體形態依然有序。

週度變化顯示，週一是延續既有漲勢，而非創造新趨勢。2 年、5 年、10 年及 30 年期殖利率在1週內分別下降 8、11、9 及 6 個基點。以1個月計，降幅分別為 2、8、12 及 15 個基點。因此，單日走勢以殖利率曲線中段最為顯著，月度走勢則仍以長端最強。這項對比顯示，最新油市消息對預期約束時點的影響，大於對長期通膨及融資風險存量的影響。

實質殖利率引領名目債券上漲。10 年期 TIPS 殖利率下降 2 個基點至 2.15%，10 年期損益兩平通膨率則上升 1 個基點至 2.32%。5 年期實質殖利率下降 4 個基點至 1.78%，7 年期下降 2 個基點至 1.96%；20 年及 30 年期實質殖利率各下降 1 個基點，分別至 2.52% 及 2.72%。實質利率下降反映立即採取央行預防性行動的必要性降低。損益兩平通膨率上升則顯示，市場並未將原油轉便宜解讀為累積通膨壓力已明確逆轉。

紐約州製造業調查強化了上述解讀中的成長面向，但價格壓力依然可見。整體指數由 19.6 降至 6月的 5.7，低於公開日程所列的 12.5 至 14.0 區間。新訂單由 22.7 降至 3.5，出貨由 18.9 降至 8.6。不過，就業由 8.3 改善至 9.6，支付價格仍高達 61.0。供給可得性進一步惡化，預期收取價格則由 43.6 升至 51.6。這份調查顯示動能放緩，而非需求或定價能力突然崩跌。

實體生產數據呈現類似的組合。5月工業生產成長 0.1%，低於 0.2%的市場共識，4月數值則修正為成長 0.9%。製造業表現持平，不及成長 0.3%的預期，製造業產能利用率維持 75.7%。總產能利用率小幅升至符合預期的 76.2%，採礦業增加 1.3%，企業設備增加 0.6%，建築用品上升 1.1%。疲弱程度集中到足以支持中期實質殖利率下降，但廣泛閒置產能並未出現，限制了消除緊縮風險的理由。

盤中價格走勢暴露了這項限制。2 年期殖利率曾低至 4.01%，10 年期曾觸及 4.42%，隨後均回吐大部分走勢。投資人歡迎荷莫茲海峽可能重新開放，但對尚未解決的核議題、制裁及航運條款仍保持警戒。收盤定價仍保留未來 Fed 升息的可觀機率，且直到 3月 2027 才完全反映1次升息。數據及油價下跌讓決策者有等待空間，但兩者均未證實目前 3.50%-3.75%的目標區間足以因應完整的傳導週期。

極短期現金殖利率依然有序。票息等值殖利率方面，4 週期維持 3.66%、6 週期維持 3.67%、8 週期維持 3.69%。13 週期殖利率上升 1 個基點至 3.72%，17 週及 26 週期分別維持 3.76% 及 3.80%，52 週期則下降 2 個基點至 3.84%。最短天期穩定，並不支持當下存在融資緊張的判斷。1 年期殖利率下降反而呼應升息急迫性降低。

國庫券標售呈現分歧的需求訊號。美國財政部接受了 $890.01億的 91 天期國庫券投標，得標利率為 3.640%，與前次發行相同，但投標倍數由 2.79 降至 2.47。間接投標人承接 49.81%，交易商吸收 45.42%。規模 $770億的 182 天期國庫券以 3.680% 得標，較前次標售低 1 個基點，投標倍數大致持平於 2.76，間接投標比例為 70.11%；交易商承接 23.33%。在沒有留存發行前交易參考值的情況下，投標倍數與配售結構是有用的比較依據。2券均於 6月18日交割，使標售結果與其後的現金市場走勢有所區隔。

## 信用、融資與初級市場承接

企業融資管道維持暢通，風險偏好轉強抵銷了美債小幅波動對上市信用債基金的機械式影響。LQD 下跌 0.0184%，HYG 上漲 0.1251%，JNK 上漲 0.1558%。高收益債指標走強與股市上漲相互呼應，但最清楚的現券市場資訊來自初級市場執行情況。週一有 8 家投資等級借款人合計發行 $360億，顯示發行人能夠大規模融資，而未擾亂整體市場走勢。

Nvidia 在自 2021 年以來首次發債中，占上述總額的 $250億。這筆 7 券別交易涵蓋 2 年至 30 年期，規模由最初約 $200億的目標上調，並吸引最高達 $850億的訂單。最長券別在銷售過程中收窄 25 個基點，最終定價為美債殖利率加 65 個基點。市場地位領先、評等強勁、鮮少發債，以及預期會計年度自由現金流超過 $2,000億，使這筆交易有別於建設支出沉重的資料中心融資。

這次發行證實，高評等 AI 曝險具有深厚需求，同時也留下中期供給疑問。Nvidia 可憑藉極強的現金創造能力借款，並將部分資金用於再融資，但其對晶片製造商與模型開發商的投資，屬於規模遠大得多的資本支出週期。高評等基金已連續 13 個月錄得資金流入，提供即時承接能力。然而，科技龍頭若反覆發債，仍會將這些流入導向一群集中的借款人；當發行日程擁擠時，也可能提高鄰近企業債及主權債存續期間的出清門檻。

加拿大的交易顯示，同一市場也能以差異化條件吸收基礎建設及營運企業風險。大蒙特婁 REM 鐵路系統透過 5 年、7 年、10 年及 30 年期債券籌集 C$18.5億。其 30 年期券殖利率為 4.791%，較政府公債高 95 個基點，訂單超過發行規模 5 倍；儘管專案成本估計達 C$94億，且超支由 Caisse 承擔，需求仍然強勁。Waste Management of Canada 發行 C$7億的 7 年期票據，定價為政府公債加 72 個基點，較初始價格指引收窄 3 個基點，以因應 9月到期的 C$5億債券。

Birkenstock 計畫在睽違 5 年後重返市場，展現信用融資能力的另一種用途。該公司正籌備 €9億的 7 年期票據，3 年後可贖回，用以置換既有債務，同時保留為額外庫藏股回購融資的選項。公司先前已宣布 $2.5億的加速回購，並表示後續回購規模最高可達 $5億。再融資可降低近期到期壓力，但加入股東回饋後，其資產負債表意義便不同於 Nvidia 現金充裕的策略性融資，或 Waste Management 單純的到期管理。

監管關注是寬鬆初級市場環境的主要反向力量。ECB 將對銀行與私募信貸連結的審查對象，從約 12 家擴大至超過 20 家放款機構。Deutsche Bank、Barclays、BNP Paribas 及 HSBC 合計占歐洲銀行對該產業曝險的近 2/3，儘管已揭露部位僅相當於貸款的約 2.3%。以目前規模而言，集中度看來尚可控管；但曝險日增，加上近期部分基金限制贖回，代表流動性轉換及銀行至基金的傳導，其重要性高於高評等債券發行表面上的良好狀況所顯示者。

## 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導

石油協議讓已開發市場央行有更多空間觀察通膨，但歐洲的政策溝通抵制全面轉鴿。ECB總裁 Christine Lagarde 表示，高能源價格的間接效應已經浮現，並強調基礎通膨及薪資傳導。存款利率才剛升至 2.25%，是自 2023 年以來第1次調升。即使原油價格大幅下跌，貨幣市場仍預期年底前再升息25個基點至 2.5%，並賦予升至 2.75%的情境 25% 機率。

歐元區活動數據說明下一步為何仍有爭議。4月工業生產僅成長 0.1%，低於 0.2%的市場共識，也低於 3月的 0.4% 增幅。資本財產出下降 0.5%，能源下降 0.4%，中間財則上升 0.8%，消費財生產表現較強。數據顯示投資疲弱、整體動能溫和，有利於存續期間。然而，ECB 持續擔憂第2輪價格效應，限制了成長疲弱轉化為前端殖利率下降的速度。

波蘭對相同能源管道作出更鴿派的回應。在 2025 年 5月至 3月期間降息 200 個基點後，央行連續第 3 個月將政策利率維持於 3.75%。決策者 Ludwik Kotecki 認為年底前沒有太多改變利率的理由，甚至可能要等到 3月 2027，因 5月通膨意外放緩至 3.1%。他將取消燃油價格上限後可能出現的任何反彈，視為行政因素，而非核心壓力重新升高的跡象。油價轉便宜強化了這種耐心觀望態度，凸顯歐洲內部的政策分歧。

台灣在週二早間報導的發展中，增添了另一項融資限制。季節性繳稅及 AI 股市熱潮相關的券商借款抽走銀行現金，使 5 年期政府公債殖利率逼近 2008 年以來高點，10 年期殖利率則接近 3 年高點。隨著放款機構保留流動性，央行定期存單餘額已降至 NT$6.15兆，為 6月 2010 以來最低。近期標售殖利率則為 2013 年以來最高。能源價格下降可以緩和通膨疑慮，卻無法補回被稅款與貸款需求吸收的現金。

這些力量使全球存續期間形成不均衡的出清機制。歐元區生產疲弱及地緣政治壓力緩解支撐債券，但 ECB 後續政策及亞洲融資趨緊，使美債以外的替代選項維持吸引力。台灣的經驗也顯示，AI 投資不僅可透過公司債發行，亦能經由銀行資產負債表連結至主權債殖利率。最長天期美債因此落後於對政策敏感的漲勢。全球儲蓄依然充裕，但動員這些資金所需的價格，取決於國內流動性、央行公信力，以及相互競爭的公私部門融資需求規模。

## 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險

美債殖利率、原油及美元走低，改善了新興市場債券的共同背景。EMB 上漲 0.3632%，將1週漲幅擴大至 1.5008%。這項上市基金漲勢反映更有利的整體風險氛圍，而非各國主權利差均獲得一致的正面判斷。各國結果仍取決於財政需求、準備金公信力及市場融資能力；對於債務管理本已可信的石油進口國，油價壓力緩解的幫助最大。

肯亞的融資計畫顯示，全球背景轉為寬鬆並不會消除再融資風險。美國財政部計畫於 2026-27 會計年度發行 $11.3億的外幣債券。連同世界銀行及 African Development Bank的預算支持，這將提供近 2/3的外部融資。當局預期以 5,290億先令的外部借款，填補相當於 GDP 5.5%的赤字。肯亞仍面臨高度債務困境風險，且已於 2月發行 $22.5億的雙券別債券；因此，新一波發行雖延續其到期管理的市場融資能力，卻也加深預算及債務清償對新增借款的依賴。

國際金融公司展示了另一條動員美元的管道。其第 2 筆新興市場貸款擔保交易，以涵蓋南美洲、中東、北非及亞洲的 62 筆美元計價貸款為基礎，籌集 $5.09億。其中 $3.2億的優先券別獲得暫定 Aaa 評等，定價為 SOFR 加 120 個基點，較第1筆交易的可比券別收窄 10 個基點。這項架構將私部門貸款風險移轉給投資人，並釋放 IFC 資本以進行新放款；這是超國家機構的風險分擔，而非主權債再融資。

辛巴威將油價下跌轉化為即時的國內政策寬鬆。央行將基準利率由 35% 降至 30%，是 ZiG 貨幣於 4月 2024 推出以來第1次降息。總裁 John Mushayavanhu 將此舉形容為重新校準，而非寬鬆週期的開始。通膨在 7月 2025 觸及 95.8% 高峰後，由 4.8% 放緩至 5月的 4.4%。荷莫茲海峽重啟可能降低燃料、肥料及食品成本，但仍極高的政策利率，反映處於早期階段的貨幣制度所承受的公信力溢價。

巴西則反向而行。央行調查將 2026 年底 Selic 預測由 13.5% 上調至 13.75%，2027 年及 2028 年預測亦向上修正。決策者已2度降息 25 個基點至 14.5%，但年底通膨預測由 5.11% 升至 5.3%，高於 3% 目標及其 4.5% 容忍區間上限。財政支持與家庭支出維持成長，使大幅寬鬆週期的空間縮小。因此，全球油價下降雖改善外部衝擊，卻未能凌駕國內通膨預期。

新興市場格局因而是共同環境轉為寬鬆，但結構性分化持續。辛巴威之所以能對能源價格下跌作出反應，是因為公布的通膨已降至個位數。肯亞仍須依靠外部借款管理龐大財政缺口，而巴西的預期利率路徑則在地緣政治壓力緩解下依然上升。IFC的交易顯示，多元化的私部門資產池能以改善中的條件吸引美元資本，但其優先順位架構及多邊機構支持，不能取代主權國家的財政調整。

## 跨資產

股市證實，美債漲勢並非對成長崩跌的防禦性反應。S&P 500 上漲 1.6528%，Nasdaq 100 上漲 3.0637%，費城半導體指數大漲 5.4456% 至歷史新高。VIX 下跌 8.3710% 至 16.20。科技股領漲反映市場重新偏好長存續期間盈餘及 AI 曝險，即使同一投資週期也帶來龐大債券發行與貸款需求。因此，實質殖利率下降與股市走強，是較有利能源衝擊的共同結果，而非彼此矛盾的衰退訊號。

能源提供最清楚的因果連結。隨著臨時協議提高市場對荷莫茲海峽供給可恢復的預期，WTI 下跌 4.8657% 至 $80.75，Brent 下跌 4.7635% 至 $83.17。避險需求減退之際，美元指數下滑 0.1203% 至 99.63。原油轉便宜降低未來通膨衝擊，美元轉弱則改善新興市場借款人的外部融資條件。風險資產同步上漲，說明美債獲得的避險需求少於單看油價走勢可能暗示的程度。

黃金使良性解讀變得複雜。即使波動率及美元下降，期貨仍上漲 2.6809% 至 $4,328。因此，對地緣政治或通膨保護的需求在整體風險偏好升溫時仍然存在。這項漲勢呼應 10 年期損益兩平通膨率單日上升及美債尾盤漲幅回吐：投資人認可近期能源結果的機率分布有所改善，但未將此框架視為最終解決方案，也未假設先前供給中斷未留下任何價格影響。

跨資產最終描繪出帶有公信力折價的有利供給面重新定價。股市、波動率、原油及美元均認同局勢降溫，高收益債基金也溫和參與。黃金、通膨補償及美債長端阻力，則使尚未解決的條款清楚可見。這種組合支持政策暫停及中期實質殖利率下降，但尚未確立持久的通膨降溫環境，也未消除 AI 投資相關的資本需求壓力。

## 前瞻市場意義

能源至 Fed的考驗，始於 6月16日 08:30 ET 公布的進出口物價指數及房市數據，接續為 6月17日 08:30的零售銷售、14:00的 FOMC 決議及 Kevin Warsh 14:30的記者會，而荷莫茲海峽預計於週五前重新開放。公開基準預期是將利率維持於 3.50%-3.75%。可觀察的觸發因素包括航運持續恢復、原油繼續下跌、貿易價格上漲廣度收窄、利率敏感需求轉弱，以及政策預測避免明確的緊縮路徑。這項組合將確認週一的實質殖利率下降解讀，並促使 2 年至 5 年期區段朝完全消除 2026 年升息的方向移動。海峽再度受阻、消費堅挺、進口價格廣泛上漲或鷹派預測，則會挑戰壓力緩解行情，並透過損益兩平通膨率及前端實質殖利率重新施壓。

6月16日的亞洲事件序列，將考驗全球對存續期間的競爭。中國於上海時間 10:00 公布 5月工業生產、零售銷售及固定資產投資；BOJ 決議隨後於尚未指定的東京時間公布，澳洲央行則於雪梨時間 14:30 宣布決策。BOJ的基準預期是由約 0.75% 升息 25 個基點至 1.00%，澳洲央行的基準預期則是維持 4.35%。可觀察的觸發因素包括 BOJ 對進一步升息及債券購買的指引、JGB 殖利率曲線與日圓的反應、中國需求廣度，以及任何澳洲央行緊縮傾向。JGB 走勢有序、匯率穩定及中國經濟活動轉弱，將挑戰海外殖利率曲線必須推高美債出清殖利率的觀點。日本長端失序拋售或政策指引同步轉鷹，則會確認投資人基礎的競爭，並對美國長天期實質殖利率構成壓力。

美債發行日程提供資產負債表考驗。6月16日 11:30 ET 標售 $650億的 42 天期國庫券，隨後於 13:00 增額發行 $130億、票息 5.000%的 20 年期公債；該債券於 6月22日交割，2046年5月15日到期。6月18日 13:00 將增額發行 $240億、票息 1.250%的 5 年期 TIPS，其後 6月19日固定收益市場假期休市。可觀察的觸發因素包括國庫券投標倍數與交易商配售、20 年期得標利率相對於當時發行前交易水準的表現、終端投資人參與度、融資穩定性，以及 FOMC 後對實質殖利率的需求。承接強勁將確認週一長端阻力與總體及政策因素有關。出現標售尾差、交易商得標比重偏高或 TIPS 需求疲弱，則會挑戰上述解讀，並使供給重新成為期限溢價壓力的獨立來源。
zh-TW.methodology_note: 除非另有註明，市場水準均以紐約收盤為準。

overall_summary.headline: 石油協議重啟由殖利率曲線中段領漲的美債行情，但長端阻力限制漲幅
overall_summary.session_narrative: 已完成的紐約收盤顯示，漲勢集中於前端及殖利率曲線中段，最長天期債券則在盤中較大漲幅消退後收平。美國與伊朗的臨時協議壓低原油價格，也降低市場認為 Fed 近期須採取預防性行動的必要性，製造業數據偏弱則進一步支持耐心觀望。通膨補償上升、股市具韌性及初級市場發行龐大，均限制了漲勢，長天期的期限溢價疑慮依然未解。
overall_summary.summary: 石油協議、生產數據轉弱及實質殖利率下降，減輕了近期政策壓力，美債因而溫和牛市趨陡。強勁的風險偏好與黏著的通膨訊號，使走勢未能擴大為全面性的存續期間行情。

section_summaries.market_read: 收盤走勢有利於中期政策敏感度，同時保留高實質利率對長端的制約。
section_summaries.next_check: 航運持續恢復及更廣泛的通膨降溫，必須證實能源壓力緩解可以延續。
section_summaries.summary: 石油協議、生產訊號轉弱及實質殖利率下降，提振了對政策敏感的美債，形成溫和牛市趨陡，而非明確的存續期間行情。股市強勁、通膨訊號持續，加上長債收平，均顯示能源轉便宜雖為政策爭取到時間，卻未化解累積的價格或融資壓力。

section_summaries.market_read: 殖利率曲線走勢反映中期政策路徑重新定價，並非長期通膨風險已乾淨消除。
section_summaries.next_check: Fed 預測與前端定價必須顯示剩餘的升息風險是否已消除。
section_summaries.section_title: 美債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構
section_summaries.summary: 前端及殖利率曲線中段領漲，反映中期實質殖利率下降及 Fed 升息的急迫性降低；與此同時，損益兩平通膨率微升，長債表現落後。製造業動能疲弱支持耐心觀望，但價格指標偏高、閒置產能有限及尾盤漲幅回吐，使緊縮風險依然存在。

section_summaries.market_read: 信用承接力依然強勁，但集中供給若一再出現，可能提高整體市場的出清門檻。
section_summaries.next_check: 隨著大型發行日程延續，終端投資人需求與交易商配售必須保持穩健。
section_summaries.section_title: 信用、融資與初級市場承接
section_summaries.summary: 初級市場消化了龐大的投資等級發行日程，其中以 Nvidia 擴大規模的多券別交易為首；高收益債指標則隨股市上漲。深厚需求維持融資管道暢通，但 AI 借款集中、為股東回饋籌資的發債，以及銀行與私募信貸日益擴大的連結，仍保留供給及流動性傳導風險。

section_summaries.market_read: 海外存續期間對儲蓄資金的競爭並不均衡，因而限制美國長端漲勢。
section_summaries.next_check: JGB 殖利率曲線穩定性、政策指引及亞洲流動性，將考驗全球存續期間需求。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導
section_summaries.summary: 油價壓力緩解對全球債券的提振並不均衡：歐元區生產疲弱有利存續期間，但 ECB 對第2輪通膨的疑慮抑制了鴿派轉向。波蘭耐心觀望凸顯政策分歧，而美國長端表現落後則顯示，面對相互競爭的政策與融資條件，全球儲蓄仍要求獲得補償。

section_summaries.market_read: 較寬鬆的共同環境支撐新興市場債券，但公信力與再融資需求仍主導分化。
section_summaries.next_check: 肯亞的市場融資能力及巴西通膨預期，將考驗新興市場壓力緩解的持久性。
section_summaries.section_title: 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險
section_summaries.summary: 美債殖利率、原油與美元走低，改善了整體新興市場環境，但各主權債表現仍然分化。辛巴威在通膨下降之際降息，肯亞仍依賴新增外部借款，巴西預期利率路徑上升，而 IFC的貸款證券化雖動員資本，卻不能取代財政調整。

section_summaries.market_read: 跨資產走勢認同局勢降溫，但尚未證實通膨與地緣政治風險已獲解決。
section_summaries.next_check: 若原油持續走弱而通膨補償維持穩定，將強化供給壓力緩解的訊號。
section_summaries.summary: 原油與美元下跌之際，風險資產上漲，證實這是有利的供給面重新定價，而非防禦性的成長恐慌。波動率下降與科技股走強呼應實質殖利率下降，但黃金、轉強的損益兩平通膨率及美債長端阻力，仍保留公信力折價。

section_summaries.market_read: 這波漲勢目前有賴政策、海外存續期間及資產負債表需求等面向共同確認。
section_summaries.next_check: 航運、央行指引及標售結果，是下一批可觀察的確認點。
section_summaries.summary: 3項考驗將決定漲勢能否擴大：能源價格向 Fed 定價的傳導、亞洲央行與殖利率曲線表現，以及美債供給的承接。航運持續恢復、海外存續期間走勢有序及標售需求強勁，將驗證中期實質殖利率下降；干擾再起、鷹派指引或投標疲弱，則會使壓力重現。

watch_events.market_implication: 若獲確認，殖利率曲線中段的升息風險將消除；若遭否定，損益兩平通膨率及前端實質殖利率將上升。
watch_events.market_trigger: 航運持續恢復且數據疲弱，將確認壓力緩解；受阻、需求堅挺或鷹派指引則予以否定。
watch_events.title: 能源至 Fed的確認
watch_events.watch_line: 關注貿易價格、房市、零售銷售、FOMC 指引，以及預定重啟的荷莫茲海峽航運。

watch_events.market_implication: 競爭緩和將支撐美國長天期債券需求；市場失序則會抬高美債實質殖利率的出清水準。
watch_events.market_trigger: JGB 走勢有序且中國轉弱，將緩和競爭；長端拋售或同步轉鷹則會加劇競爭。
watch_events.title: 亞洲存續期間競爭
watch_events.watch_line: 關注 BOJ 指引、JGB 殖利率曲線與日圓、中國需求廣度及澳洲央行政策傾向。

watch_events.market_implication: 承接良好代表阻力仍由總體因素主導；投標疲弱則會恢復供給驅動的期限溢價壓力。
watch_events.market_trigger: 終端投資人需求強勁將確認承接力；出現標售尾差、交易商得標比重偏高或 TIPS 需求疲弱則予以否定。
watch_events.title: 美債資產負債表考驗
watch_events.watch_line: 關注國庫券投標倍數、20 年期標售執行情況、終端投資人需求及 5 年期 TIPS 投標。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

The June 15 close reopened the oil-relief rally that had failed to extend at the end of the prior week, but only in its most policy-sensitive form. Treasuries richened through the belly as the US-Iran interim agreement pulled crude lower and reduced the perceived need for a near-term Federal Reserve response. The long bond did not participate, and much larger intraday gains faded before the close. The result was a modest bull steepening rather than a decisive escape from the high-real-rate regime.

That outcome changed the June 12 interpretation without reversing it completely. Friday’s close had rebuilt policy premium even as oil fell, because resilient risk appetite and concern about lagged inflation outweighed geopolitical relief. Monday added a firmer agreement, another decline in energy prices and weaker US production data. Traders reduced the probability of a quarter-point Fed increase by December from fully priced during the previous week to roughly 74%. Even so, the remaining probability and the late-session Treasury retracement showed that the market still regarded cheaper spot oil as relief from an acute shock, not evidence that accumulated price pressure had disappeared.

3 transmission channels defined the close. The agreement reduced the energy tail and lowered real yields, especially in the 5-year sector. US manufacturing indicators then reinforced the case for patience, although elevated survey price measures, stable capacity utilization and a small rise in 10-year inflation compensation prevented a dovish reading. Finally, strong equities and heavy absorption of a jumbo Nvidia bond sale showed that the rally was not driven by a growth scare or impaired credit creation. Risk appetite remained capable of financing AI-related capital demand even as that demand preserved competition for savings.

The counterforce came from duration outside the immediate policy window. European officials continued to emphasize second-round energy effects, Taiwan’s funding squeeze was lifting local clearing yields, and the longest Treasury maturity was unchanged. The close therefore restored part of June 11’s de-escalation logic while preserving June 12’s central warning: an oil agreement can buy central banks time, but durable fixed-income relief still depends on realized supply normalization, broader disinflation and evidence that public and private issuance can clear without raising the term premium.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

Treasury gains were concentrated in the front and belly. The 2-year yield fell 2 basis points to 4.07%, the 5-year declined 3 to 4.18%, and the 10-year eased 1 to 4.47%. The 30-year yield was unchanged at 4.97%. This maturity pattern located the adjustment in expected policy restraint and intermediate real rates. It did not show a comparable reduction in the compensation required for the longest duration, where fiscal supply, global alternatives and persistent inflation uncertainty remain more influential.

The curve bull-steepened modestly. The 2s10s slope widened 1 basis point to 40, and 2s30s widened 2 to 90. The 2s5s10s butterfly fell 3 basis points to -18 as the 5-year sector outperformed both wings, while the 2s10s30s butterfly held at -10. That combination is consistent with a repricing of the medium-term policy path rather than a standalone rally in the long bond. It also left the curve’s broader shape orderly despite the geopolitical catalyst.

Weekly changes show that Monday extended an existing rally instead of creating a new trend. 2-, 5-, 10- and 30-year yields were lower by 8, 11, 9 and 6 basis points over 1 week. Over 1 month, the declines were 2, 8, 12 and 15 basis points. The daily move was therefore most pronounced in the belly, whereas the monthly move remained strongest at the long end. The contrast indicates that the latest oil news changed the expected timing of restraint more than the longer-run stock of inflation and financing risk.

Real yields led the nominal rally. The 10-year TIPS yield fell 2 basis points to 2.15%, while the 10-year breakeven rose 1 basis point to 2.32%. 5-year real yields dropped 4 basis points to 1.78%, and 7-year real yields declined 2 to 1.96%; 20- and 30-year real yields each fell 1 basis point, to 2.52% and 2.72%. Lower real rates captured a reduced need for immediate central-bank insurance. The positive breakeven move showed that the market had not translated cheaper crude into a clean reversal of accumulated inflation pressure.

The Empire State survey strengthened the growth side of that interpretation but left price pressure visible. The headline fell to 5.7 in June from 19.6, below the consensus range of 12.5 to 14.0. New orders dropped to 3.5 from 22.7 and shipments to 8.6 from 18.9. Employment improved to 9.6 from 8.3, however, and prices paid remained elevated at 61.0. Supply availability deteriorated further, while expected prices received rose to 51.6 from 43.6. The survey argued for slower momentum, not an abrupt collapse in demand or pricing power.

Hard production data delivered a similar mix. Industrial production rose 0.1% in May, below the 0.2% consensus, after April was revised to a 0.9% increase. Manufacturing was flat against a 0.3% expectation, and manufacturing utilization held at 75.7%. Total capacity utilization edged up to the expected 76.2%, mining increased 1.3%, business equipment gained 0.6% and construction supplies rose 1.1%. The softness was concentrated enough to justify lower intermediate real yields, but the absence of broad slack limited the case for removing tightening risk.

Intraday price action exposed that limit. The 2-year yield fell as low as 4.01% and the 10-year reached 4.42% before each retraced most of the move. Investors welcomed the prospective reopening of the Strait of Hormuz but remained wary of unresolved nuclear, sanctions and shipping terms. The close retained a substantial chance of a future Fed increase and did not fully price one until March 2027. The data and oil decline gave policymakers room to wait; neither established that the current 3.50%-3.75% target range will be sufficient through the full pass-through cycle.

Very short cash yields remained orderly. Coupon-equivalent yields were unchanged at 3.66% for 4 weeks, 3.67% for 6 weeks and 3.69% for 8 weeks. The 13-week yield rose 1 basis point to 3.72%, the 17- and 26-week yields held at 3.76% and 3.80%, and the 52-week yield fell 2 basis points to 3.84%. Stability at the shortest maturities argues against a contemporaneous funding squeeze. The 1-year decline instead echoed the reduced urgency of a policy increase.

Bill auctions produced a split demand signal. Treasury accepted $89.001 billion of 91-day bills at 3.640%, unchanged from the prior sale, but cover fell to 2.47 from 2.79. Indirect bidders took 49.81%, and dealers absorbed 45.42%. The $77.000 billion 182-day sale cleared at 3.680%, 1 basis point below the prior auction, with cover nearly unchanged at 2.76 and indirect participation of 70.11%; dealers took 23.33%. Cover and bidder allocation provide the useful comparison; without a contemporaneous when-issued yield, no tail inference is possible. Both issues settle June 18, keeping the auction result distinct from the later cash movement.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

Corporate funding remained open, with stronger risk appetite offsetting the mechanical effect of modest Treasury moves on listed credit funds. LQD slipped 0.0184%, while HYG rose 0.1251% and JNK gained 0.1558%. The firmer high-yield gauges aligned with the equity rally, but the clearest cash-market information came from primary execution. 8 investment-grade borrowers sold a combined $36 billion on Monday, showing that issuers could access size without destabilizing the broader tape.

Nvidia accounted for $25 billion of that total in its 1st bond offering since 2021. The 7-part sale spanned 2 to 30 years, was increased from an initial target of about $20 billion and drew as much as $85 billion of orders. The longest tranche tightened by 25 basis points during marketing to 65 basis points over Treasuries. A dominant market position, strong ratings, rarity as an issuer and expected fiscal-year free cash flow above $200 billion distinguished the transaction from construction-heavy data-center finance.

The execution confirmed deep demand for high-grade AI exposure while preserving a medium-term supply question. Nvidia can borrow against exceptional cash generation and use proceeds partly to refinance debt, yet its investments across chipmakers and model developers sit within a much larger capital cycle. High-grade funds had recorded 13 consecutive months of inflows, providing immediate absorption capacity. Repeated issuance by technology leaders still channels those inflows toward a concentrated set of borrowers and can raise the clearing hurdle for adjacent corporate and sovereign duration when calendars become crowded.

Canadian deals showed that the same market could absorb infrastructure and operating-company risk at differentiated terms. Greater Montreal’s REM rail system raised C$1.85 billion across 5-, 7-, 10- and 30-year maturities. Its 30-year tranche yielded 4.791% at 95 basis points over government bonds and drew orders exceeding 5 times its size, despite project costs estimated at C$9.4 billion and the Caisse bearing overruns. Waste Management of Canada sold C$700 million of 7-year notes at 72 basis points over governments, 3 basis points tighter than initial pricing, ahead of a C$500 million bond maturity in September.

Birkenstock’s planned return after 5 years illustrated a different use of credit capacity. The company was preparing €900 million of 7-year notes callable after 3 years to replace existing debt while retaining the option to fund additional share repurchases. It had already announced a $250 million accelerated repurchase and said further purchases could reach $500 million. Refinancing reduces near-term maturity pressure, but adding shareholder distributions changes the balance-sheet interpretation relative to Nvidia’s cash-rich strategic financing or Waste Management’s straightforward maturity management.

Supervisory attention supplied the main counterforce to easy primary conditions. The ECB expanded its review of bank links to private credit to more than 20 lenders from about a dozen. Deutsche Bank, Barclays, BNP Paribas and HSBC account for nearly 2/3 of European bank exposure to the sector, although disclosed positions equal only about 2.3% of loans. The concentration looks manageable at current scale, yet growing exposures and recent withdrawal limits at some funds make liquidity transformation and bank-to-fund transmission more important than the benign surface of high-grade issuance suggests.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

The oil agreement gave developed-market central banks more room to observe inflation, but European policy communication resisted a full dovish pivot. ECB President Christine Lagarde said indirect effects from high energy prices had already appeared and emphasized underlying inflation and wage transmission. The deposit rate had just been raised to 2.25%, its 1st increase since 2023. Money markets still expected another quarter-point increase to 2.5% by year-end and assigned a 25% chance to 2.75%, even after the sharp decline in crude.

Euro-area activity data explained why the next step is contested. Industrial production rose only 0.1% in April, below the 0.2% consensus and down from March’s 0.4% increase. Capital-goods output fell 0.5% and energy declined 0.4%, while intermediate goods rose 0.8% and consumer-goods production was stronger. The release points to weak investment and modest aggregate momentum, which supports duration. Continued ECB concern about second-round prices limits how quickly that growth softness can translate into lower front-end yields.

Poland showed a more dovish response to the same energy channel. The central bank held its key rate at 3.75% for a 3rd month after reducing it by 200 basis points between May 2025 and March. Policymaker Ludwik Kotecki saw little reason to change rates before year-end, and possibly before March 2027, after May inflation slowed unexpectedly to 3.1%. He treated any rebound from the removal of a fuel-price cap as administrative rather than a sign of renewed core pressure. Cheaper oil strengthened that patience, highlighting policy divergence inside Europe.

Taiwan added a separate funding constraint in developments reported early Tuesday. Seasonal tax payments and brokerage borrowing tied to the AI equity boom had drained bank cash, pushing 5-year government yields near their highest since 2008 and 10-year yields close to a 3-year peak. Central-bank certificate-of-deposit balances had fallen to NT$6.15 trillion, the lowest since June 2010, as lenders conserved liquidity. Recent auction yields were the highest since 2013. Lower energy prices can temper inflation concern, but they do not replenish cash absorbed by taxes and loan demand.

These forces left global duration with an uneven clearing mechanism. Weak euro-area production and geopolitical relief supported bonds, whereas ECB follow-through and tighter Asian funding maintained attractive alternatives to Treasuries. Taiwan’s experience also linked AI investment to sovereign yields through bank balance sheets rather than corporate issuance alone. The longest US maturities consequently lagged the policy-sensitive rally. Global savings remained available, but the price required to mobilize them depended on domestic liquidity, central-bank credibility and the volume of competing public and private claims.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

The common backdrop improved for emerging-market debt as Treasury yields, crude and the dollar moved lower. EMB gained 0.3632%, extending its 1-week increase to 1.5008%. That listed-fund advance captured a more favorable broad risk tone rather than a uniform sovereign-spread verdict. Country outcomes remained governed by fiscal needs, reserve credibility and market access, with oil relief helping importers most where debt management was already credible.

Kenya’s financing plan showed why a softer global backdrop does not remove refinancing risk. The Treasury plans a $1.13 billion foreign bond for fiscal 2026-27. Together with World Bank and African Development Bank budget support, it would provide nearly 2/3 of external financing. Authorities expect 529 billion shillings of foreign borrowing to cover a deficit equal to 5.5% of GDP. Kenya remains at high risk of debt distress and raised $2.25 billion in dual-tranche notes in February, so the new sale extends maturity-management access while deepening reliance on fresh borrowing for both budgets and debt service.

The International Finance Corp. demonstrated a different dollar-mobilization channel. Its 2nd emerging-market loan-backed transaction raised $509 million against 62 dollar-denominated loans across South America, the Middle East, North Africa and Asia. A $320 million senior tranche carried a provisional Aaa rating and priced at 120 basis points over SOFR, 10 basis points tighter than the comparable tranche in the 1st transaction. The structure transfers private-sector loan risk to investors and releases IFC capital for new lending; it is supranational risk sharing, not sovereign refinancing.

Zimbabwe converted the oil decline into immediate domestic policy relief. The central bank cut its benchmark rate to 30% from 35%, the 1st reduction since the ZiG currency was introduced in April 2024. Governor John Mushayavanhu described the move as a realignment rather than the start of an easing cycle. Inflation slowed to 4.4% in May from 4.8%, after peaking at 95.8% in July 2025. Reopening Hormuz could lower fuel, fertilizer and food costs, but the still-extreme policy rate reflects the credibility premium attached to a currency regime in its early life.

Brazil moved in the opposite direction. The central-bank survey raised the end-2026 Selic forecast to 13.75% from 13.5%, with projections for 2027 and 2028 also revised higher. Policymakers had cut twice by 25 basis points to 14.5%, yet the year-end inflation forecast rose to 5.3% from 5.11%, above the 3% target and its 4.5% upper tolerance. Fiscal support and household spending were sustaining growth, narrowing the space for an aggressive easing cycle. Lower global oil therefore improved the external impulse without overruling domestic inflation expectations.

The EM picture was consequently one of easier common conditions and persistent structural dispersion. Zimbabwe could respond to falling energy prices because reported inflation had already moved into single digits. Kenya still needed external borrowing to manage a large fiscal gap, and Brazil’s expected rate path rose despite geopolitical relief. IFC’s transaction showed that diversified private-sector pools can attract dollar capital on improving terms, but its senior structure and multilateral sponsorship are not substitutes for sovereign fiscal adjustment.

## Cross asset

Equities confirmed that the Treasury rally was not a defensive response to collapsing growth. The S&P 500 gained 1.6528%, the Nasdaq 100 rose 3.0637% and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index jumped 5.4456% to a record. The VIX fell 8.3710% to 16.20. Technology leadership reflected renewed appetite for long-duration earnings and AI exposure, even as the same investment cycle generated heavy bond issuance and loan demand. Lower real yields and stronger equities were therefore joint consequences of a more favorable energy shock, not opposing recession signals.

Energy supplied the clearest causal link. WTI fell 4.8657% to $80.75, and Brent declined 4.7635% to $83.17 as the interim agreement raised expectations that supply through the Strait of Hormuz could resume. The dollar index slipped 0.1203% to 99.63 as haven demand eased. Cheaper crude reduced the prospective inflation impulse, while the softer dollar improved external financing conditions for emerging borrowers. The simultaneous rally in risk assets explains why Treasuries received less haven demand than the oil move alone might have implied.

Gold complicated the benign reading. Futures rose 2.6809% to $4,328 even as volatility and the dollar declined. Demand for geopolitical or inflation protection therefore survived the broad risk-on move. The increase aligns with the positive daily change in 10-year breakevens and the late retracement in Treasuries: investors recognized a better near-term energy distribution without treating the framework as a final settlement or assuming that previous supply disruption had left no price legacy.

Cross assets ultimately described a favorable supply-side repricing with a credibility discount. Equities, volatility, crude and the dollar all endorsed de-escalation, and high-yield funds participated modestly. Gold, inflation compensation and long-end Treasury resistance kept the unresolved terms visible. The configuration supports a policy pause and lower intermediate real yields, but it does not yet establish a durable disinflation regime or remove the capital-demand pressure associated with AI investment.

## Forward Market Significance

The energy-to-Fed test runs from the June 16 import and export price indexes and housing data at 08:30 ET through June 17 retail sales at 08:30, the FOMC decision at 14:00 and Kevin Warsh’s press conference at 14:30, with the Strait of Hormuz expected to reopen by Friday. the consensus expectation is a hold at 3.50%-3.75%. Observable triggers are sustained shipping, a continued crude decline, narrower trade-price breadth, weaker interest-sensitive demand and policy projections that avoid an explicit tightening path. That combination would confirm Monday’s lower-real-yield interpretation and push the 2- to 5-year sector toward fully removing a 2026 hike. Renewed obstruction in the strait, firm consumption, broad import-price gains or hawkish projections would challenge the relief rally and reopen pressure through both breakevens and front-end real yields.

The June 16 Asian sequence tests global competition for duration. China releases May industrial production, retail sales and fixed-asset investment at 10:00 in Shanghai; the BOJ decision follows at an unspecified Tokyo time, and the RBA announces at 14:30 in Sydney. The BOJ baseline is a 25-basis-point increase from about 0.75% to 1.00%, while the RBA baseline is a hold at 4.35%. Observable triggers are the BOJ’s guidance on further hikes and bond purchases, the JGB curve and yen response, the breadth of Chinese demand, and any RBA tightening bias. Orderly JGBs, stable currencies and softer Chinese activity would challenge the view that foreign curves must lift the Treasury clearing yield. A disorderly Japanese long-end selloff or synchronized hawkish guidance would confirm buyer-base competition and pressure US long-duration real yields.

The Treasury calendar provides the balance-sheet test. On June 16, a $65 billion 42-day bill auction at 11:30 ET is followed by a $13 billion reopening of the 5.000% 20-year bond at 13:00; the bond settles June 22 and matures May 15, 2046. A $24 billion 1.250% 5-year TIPS reopening follows June 18 at 13:00 before the June 19 fixed-income holiday closes the market. Observable triggers are bill cover and dealer allocation, the 20-year stop against its then-current when-issued level, end-user participation, funding stability and demand for real yield after the FOMC. Strong absorption would confirm that Monday’s long-end resistance was macro and policy related. A tail, heavy dealer awards or weak TIPS demand would challenge that account and reopen supply as an independent source of term-premium pressure.

section_summaries.market_read: The close favored intermediate policy sensitivity while preserving a high-real-rate long-end constraint.
section_summaries.next_check: Sustained shipping and broader disinflation must confirm that the energy relief can endure.
section_summaries.summary: Policy-sensitive Treasuries benefited from the oil agreement, weaker production signals and lower real yields, producing modest bull steepening rather than a decisive duration rally. Strong equities, persistent inflation signals and an unchanged long bond showed that cheaper energy bought policy time without resolving accumulated price or financing pressure.

section_summaries.market_read: The curve move was a medium-term policy repricing, not a clean removal of long-run inflation risk.
section_summaries.next_check: Fed projections and front-end pricing must show whether the remaining increase risk is removed.
section_summaries.section_title: UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure
section_summaries.summary: Front-end and belly-led gains reflected lower intermediate real yields and reduced urgency for a Fed increase, while breakevens edged higher and the long bond lagged. Soft manufacturing momentum supported patience, but elevated price measures, limited slack and late-session retracement kept tightening risk alive.

section_summaries.market_read: Credit absorption remained strong, but repeated concentrated supply could raise the broader clearing hurdle.
section_summaries.next_check: End-user demand and dealer allocations must stay firm as large issuance calendars continue.
section_summaries.summary: Primary markets absorbed a heavy investment-grade calendar, led by Nvidia’s upsized multi-tranche deal, as high-yield gauges rose with equities. Deep demand kept funding open, yet concentrated AI borrowing, shareholder-funded issuance and expanding bank links to private credit preserved supply and liquidity-transmission risks.

section_summaries.market_read: Foreign duration remained an uneven competitor for savings, constraining the US long-end rally.
section_summaries.next_check: JGB curve stability, policy guidance and Asian liquidity will test global duration demand.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission
section_summaries.summary: Oil relief supported global bonds unevenly: weak euro-area production favored duration, but ECB concern about second-round inflation restrained a dovish pivot. Poland’s patience highlighted policy divergence, while the lagging US long end showed that global savings still required compensation for competing policy and funding conditions.

section_summaries.market_read: Easier common conditions supported EM debt, while credibility and refinancing needs drove dispersion.
section_summaries.next_check: Kenya’s market access and Brazil’s inflation expectations will test the durability of EM relief.
section_summaries.section_title: EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk
section_summaries.summary: Lower Treasury yields, crude and the dollar improved broad EM conditions, but sovereign outcomes remained dispersed. Zimbabwe eased amid lower inflation, Kenya still depended on fresh external borrowing, Brazil’s expected rate path rose, and IFC’s loan securitization mobilized capital without substituting for fiscal adjustment.

section_summaries.market_read: Cross assets endorsed de-escalation without confirming that inflation and geopolitical risks were resolved.
section_summaries.next_check: Continued crude weakness alongside stable inflation compensation would strengthen the supply-relief signal.
section_summaries.summary: Risk assets rallied as crude and the dollar fell, confirming a favorable supply-side repricing rather than a defensive growth scare. Lower volatility and stronger technology shares aligned with lower real yields, while gold, firmer breakevens and long-end Treasury resistance retained a credibility discount.

section_summaries.market_read: The rally now depends on confirmation across policy, foreign duration and balance-sheet demand.
section_summaries.next_check: Shipping, central-bank guidance and auction outcomes are the next observable confirmation points.
section_summaries.summary: 3 tests will determine whether the rally broadens: energy pass-through into Fed pricing, Asian central-bank and curve behavior, and Treasury supply absorption. Sustained shipping, orderly foreign duration and strong auction demand would validate lower intermediate real yields; renewed disruption, hawkish guidance or weak bidding would restore pressure.

methodology_note: Market levels are shown on an NY-close basis unless otherwise noted.

bullets: 10Y TIPS real yield 2.15% (-2 bp) | 10Y breakeven inflation 2.32% (+1 bp)
UST 2s10s curve 40 bp (+1 bp) | UST 2s30s curve 90 bp (+2 bp)
overall_summary.headline: Oil agreement revives a belly-led Treasury rally, but long-end resistance limits relief
overall_summary.session_narrative: The completed NY close showed gains concentrated in the front end and belly, with the longest maturity unchanged after a larger intraday rally faded. The US-Iran interim agreement lowered crude and the perceived need for near-term Fed insurance, while softer manufacturing data reinforced patience. Rising inflation compensation, resilient equities and heavy primary issuance limited the move, leaving long-duration term-premium concerns intact.
overall_summary.summary: Treasuries bull-steepened modestly as the oil agreement, weaker production data and lower real yields reduced near-term policy pressure. Strong risk appetite and sticky inflation signals kept the move from becoming a broad duration rally.

watch_events.market_implication: Confirmation removes hike risk from the belly; rejection lifts breakevens and front-end real yields.
watch_events.market_trigger: Sustained shipping and soft data confirm relief; obstruction, firm demand or hawkish guidance reject it.
watch_events.title: Energy-to-Fed Confirmation
watch_events.watch_line: Watch trade prices, housing, retail sales, FOMC guidance and the scheduled Hormuz reopening.

watch_events.market_implication: Easing supports US long-duration demand; disorder raises the Treasury real-yield clearing level.
watch_events.market_trigger: Orderly JGBs and softer China ease competition; a long-end selloff or joint hawkishness intensifies it.
watch_events.title: Asian Duration Competition
watch_events.watch_line: Watch BOJ guidance, the JGB curve and yen, Chinese demand breadth and the RBA bias.

watch_events.market_implication: Good absorption keeps resistance macro-led; weak bidding restores supply-driven term-premium pressure.
watch_events.market_trigger: Strong end-user demand confirms absorption; a tail, heavy dealer awards or weak TIPS demand rejects it.
watch_events.title: Treasury Balance-Sheet Test
watch_events.watch_line: Watch bill cover, 20-year auction execution, end-user demand and 5-year TIPS bidding.
